rows.source_ref: Gloriar (handle), thread "Forex My Passion - Ashi Strategy (Intraday & Scalping)", BabyPips forum, 2012-2013, https://forums.babypips.com/t/forex-my-passion-ashi-strategy-intraday-scalping/103326
rows.concept: Enter in the direction of a Heiken-Ashi-Smoothed trend only when price closes outside a 14-Envelope and ADX>22 with AO and MACD both confirming the same side, i.e. a multi-indicator momentum-confluence breakout of a volatility band.
rows.eligibility_reason: Deterministic entry from native/custom MQL5 indicators (Envelopes, iADX, iAO, iMACD, Heiken-Ashi-Smoothed), SL = 5 pips beyond swing high/low, FX majors available on .DWX; exit/TP are discretionary in the thread but mechanizable (opposite-close-back-inside-envelope exit + fixed pip target).
rows.notes: Two variants share ONE core rule set: (1) Intraday H1/Daily targeting 5-30 pips; (2) Scalping M30/H1 that re-enters for 5-20 pips while Heiken Ashi stays same color and price stays outside the envelope. Pairs tested: AUD/USD, EUR/USD, GBP/USD, USD/CHF (author also tried GBP/JPY, gold, silver). Envelope: MA Simple, Apply-to-Close, Deviation 0.10%. Generic indicator-soup FX trend follower with no structural/limit-to-arbitrage edge and highly curve-fittable parameter stack -> low conviction per FX-edge doctrine; entry is deterministic but TP/SL are admitted to be discretionary ('grey area', 'being lazy' on SL). Anecdotal forum evidence only, no verified backtest. Author also mentions an undescribed 'London Breakout' cheesy system in-progress -> no rules stated, not a usable candidate.
rows.notes_secondary: NewNine suggests Schaff Trend Cycle as MACD substitute; not part of core rules.

notes: single BabyPips thread; intraday + scalping are variants of one core rule set -> one row
